synthesis.summary: MU surged ~9.7% today but none of the supplied news items contain substantive information explaining the move — both are generic market commentary with no disclosed catalyst. The gain likely stems from options flow, sector rotation into memory/AI chip names, or a company-specific event (e.g., earnings, guidance, or analyst action) not captured in the provided headlines.

IONQ surged 10.58% with no high-impact news to explain the move. The only supplied news item is a low-impact rumor from Martin Shkreli alleging misleading claims by the company — this is negative framing and does not account for a double-digit rally. The move is likely driven by options flow, short covering, or sector rotation in quantum computing names, none of which are visible in the supplied data.

CSCO is up 2.07% today with no supplied news that explains the move. Volume is below average (rel_volume 0.86), suggesting this is not a high-conviction institutional drive, and is more likely attributable to broad sector rotation or general market momentum rather than any company-specific catalyst.
